title: Delete Constraint
text: Delete Constraint Defines Uniface behavior when an occurrence of the One Entity is deleted, and there are related occurrences of the many entity available in the component data structure. Valid values are Restricted (default), Cascading , or Nullify . Value Description Restricted An occurrence of the One Entity cannot be deleted if there are occurrences of the Many Entity with a matching foreign key. Cascading If an occurrence of the One Entity is deleted, all occurrences in the Many Entity that have a matching foreign keys are also deleted. Nullify If an occurrence of the One Entity is deleted, the foreign key value of occurrences in the Many Entity that have a matching foreign key is set to NULL. Applies To Relationship Related Links Referential Integrity Referential integrity is a standard feature of relational database design that prevents users or applications from entering inconsistent data. Most relational databases have referential integrity rules that you can apply when you create a relationship between tables. Relationships A relationship is an association between one entity and another. It is modeled as a development object belonging to the many-side entity in a one-to-many relationship.
